question_groups.instruction: Questions 1–4 Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

items.prompt_html: Dr Engel has spent ten years gathering evidence for the notion that animals treat their own illnesses.
Animal behaviourists have always believed that animals know how to heal themselves.
Dr Karesh enlisted the help of a medical team during his research.
Wild animals taken into captivity tend to experience a decline in health.

options.label: TRUE
options.text: if the statement agrees with the information

options.label: FALSE
options.text: if the statement contradicts the information

options.label: NOT GIVEN
options.text: if there is no information on this

question_groups.prompt_html:
Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1 Dr Engel has spent ten years gathering evidence for the notion that animals treat their own illnesses.

2 Animal behaviourists have always believed that animals know how to heal themselves.

3 Dr Karesh enlisted the help of a medical team during his research.

4 Wild animals taken into captivity tend to experience a decline in health.

Complete the table below.

ResearchersSubjectsSelf-treatmentFindingsOther observations
Micheal Huffman & Mohamedi Seifu 1987Chimps with wormsEat <blank data-qid="q5"/>of Veronia plantPlant contains toxins known as <blank data-qid="q6"/>that kill parasites.Remedy also used by people
James Gilardi 1999Macaws that consume poison-bearing seedsEat clayClay leads to reduction in level of <blank data-qid="q7"/>in blood
Richard WranghamChimps with gut parasitesEat Aspilia leavesUndigested leaves wrongly believed to contain a <blank data-qid="q8"/>Chimps indicate leaves have an <blank data-qid="q9"/>taste

Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

question_groups.instruction: Questions 10–13 Complete the summary below. Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer. Soil as medication The consumption of soil for medicinal reasons is called 10 ___________ .Initially, people believed that animals ate dirt to get 11 ___________ and other minerals. They then realised that the soil eaten in some areas did not contain a lot of minerals, and that in other areas plants were a better mineral source. Many people believe that the clay in soil can 12 ___________ poisons. Indeed, clay- based 13 ___________ are sold in African markets to treat a range of illnesses.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Soil as medication

The consumption of soil for medicinal reasons is called <blank data-qid="q10"/>.Initially, people believed that animals ate dirt to get <blank data-qid="q11"/>and other minerals. They then realised that the soil eaten in some areas did not contain a lot of minerals, and that in other areas plants were a better mineral source. Many people believe that the clay in soil can <blank data-qid="q12"/>poisons. Indeed, clay- based <blank data-qid="q13"/>are sold in African markets to treat a range of illnesses.

question_groups.instruction: Questions 14–19 Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below. Choose the correct number, i-ix , in boxes 14-19 . List of Headings

options.label: i
options.text: How sounds and physical contact strengthen social connections

options.label: ii
options.text: A cross-cultural study of superficial interaction

options.label: iii
options.text: An experiment that led to surprising results

options.label: iv
options.text: A study that categorised types of spoken exchanges

options.label: v
options.text: The personality types that find small talk more difficult

options.label: vi
options.text: The effect of social media on conversation skills

options.label: vii
options.text: Some occupations involve little or no interesting conversation

options.label: viii
options.text: A campaign to improve interaction in the workplace

options.label: ix
options.text: A range of common situations that were enhanced by small talk

question_groups.prompt_html:
Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs.

Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.

Choose the correct number, i-ix , in boxes 14-19 .

List of Headings

i How sounds and physical contact strengthen social connections

ii A cross-cultural study of superficial interaction

iii An experiment that led to surprising results

iv A study that categorised types of spoken exchanges

v The personality types that find small talk more difficult

vi The effect of social media on conversation skills

vii Some occupations involve little or no interesting conversation

viii A campaign to improve interaction in the workplace

ix A range of common situations that were enhanced by small talk

question_groups.instruction: Questions 20–23 Look at the following statements ( Questions 20-23 ) and the list of people below. Match each statement with the correct person, A-E . Choose the correct letter, A-E , in boxes 20-23 . NB: You may use any letter more than once.

items.prompt_html: Interacting socially is preferable to hurrying through a transaction.
People whose conversations are mainly of a personal and meaningful nature are more satisfied with their lives.
There is more than one species whose members communicate for social reasons.
Some people find it easier than others to change the subject of a conversation.

options.label: A
options.text: Matthias R. Mehl and Simine Vazire

options.label: B
options.text: Ipek Kulahci

options.label: C
options.text: Nicholas Epley and Juliana Schroeder

options.label: D
options.text: Gillian Sandstrom and Elizabeth Dunn

options.label: E
options.text: Todd Kashdan

question_groups.prompt_html:
Look at the following statements ( Questions 20-23 ) and the list of people below.

Match each statement with the correct person, A-E .

Choose the correct letter, A-E , in boxes 20-23 .

NB: You may use any letter more than once.

List of Researchers

A . Matthias R. Mehl and Simine Vazire

B . Ipek Kulahci

C . Nicholas Epley and Juliana Schroeder

D . Gillian Sandstrom and Elizabeth Dunn

E . Todd Kashdan

20 Interacting socially is preferable to hurrying through a transaction.

21 People whose conversations are mainly of a personal and meaningful nature are more satisfied with their lives.

22 There is more than one species whose members communicate for social reasons.

23 Some people find it easier than others to change the subject of a conversation.

Introverted people found it helpful to be given 26 ____________ which could use to start a conversation.

items.prompt_html: Which aspect of American business was admired?

options.label: A
options.text: the high level of employment

options.label: B
options.text: the variety of products available

options.label: C
options.text: the methods of increasing productivity

options.label: D
options.text: the quality of the goods that were produced

items.prompt_html: The continuous working week was intended to ensure that

options.label: A
options.text: each worker's productivity increased.

options.label: B
options.text: machinery was in continuous use.

options.label: C
options.text: workers had more days off.

options.label: D
options.text: factories could afford to buy efficient machines.

items.prompt_html: Which of the following was thought unlikely to improve as a result of the continuous working week?

options.label: A
options.text: family life

options.label: B
options.text: leisure facilities

options.label: C
options.text: employment levels

options.label: D
options.text: shopping opportunities
